events.title: People in Plazas – Free Lunchtime Music
events.description: Free outdoor lunchtime concerts at plazas on and near Market Street (Embarcadero to 4th St). Runs Tuesdays and Thursdays through September 2026. 130+ concerts featuring local musicians.

events.title: Timmy No Brakes
events.description: Stand-up comedian Timmy No Brakes performs at Cobb's Comedy Club, 915 Columbus Ave.

events.title: Liz Miele
events.description: Stand-up comedian Liz Miele, known for her neurotic, intellectual humor (Comedy Central, FOX, NPR), performs a run of shows July 8–11 at Punch Line SF.

events.title: Bachata Party with Inessence Dance Co.
events.description: Bachata dance social at Cigar Bar and Grill (850 Montgomery St) with Inessence Dance Co.

events.title: People in Plazas – Free Lunchtime Music
events.description: Free outdoor lunchtime concerts at plazas on and near Market Street. Runs Tuesdays and Thursdays through September 2026.

events.title: EMM
events.description: Live performance by EMM with support from Bentley Robles and TeZATalks at The Chapel. Doors at 8 PM.

events.title: Dance Fridays – Salsa & Bachata
events.description: Weekly SF Latin dance party: beginner lessons at 7:45 PM, then social dancing until 1:30 AM across two rooms. 21+.

events.title: EL 7HARA: Arabic Global Dance Party
events.cost_notes: check Eventbrite; proceeds to Salam Charity
events.description: DJ Juicy Baklawa and DJ Ahmed spin Arabic beats and international global music. Proceeds benefit humanitarian fundraising through Salam Charity.

events.title: James Austin Johnson
events.description: SNL cast member James Austin Johnson, celebrated for his dead-on political impressions, performs a weekend run at Cobb's July 10–11.

events.title: Liz Miele
events.description: Liz Miele continues her Punch Line SF run with two shows on Friday July 10 (7:30 PM and 9:45 PM).

events.title: Salsa Night with Live Band
events.description: Salsa night with live band at Cigar Bar and Grill, 850 Montgomery St.

events.title: Minnesota Street Project First Saturday
events.description: Monthly celebration of exhibition openings, artist talks, and book signings across 10+ contemporary art spaces. Open to all. Current shows include 'Memory Cradle' (Eleanor Harwood Gallery, through Jul 18), 'Mark Perlman — Recent Works' (Nancy Toomey, through Jul 18), and ICA SF's 'Tara Donovan: Stratagems' (through Jul 31).

events.title: Ryan Graff Contemporary: The Backwards Race — Opening
events.description: Gallery Night opening reception for 'The Backwards Race' by Aina Clotas at Ryan Graff Contemporary, 804 Sutter Street. 5–9 PM.

events.title: BATS Improv: Super Scene
events.description: BATS Improv: Five stories start, only one ends. Saturday improv performance at Fort Mason Center, Building B.

events.title: San Francisco Free Folk Festival
events.description: 50th annual SF Free Folk Festival featuring 22 performances and 18 workshops across multiple stages with folk, Irish, Cajun-Creole, jazz, blues, sea shanties, and storytelling. Runs 11 AM–6 PM, all ages.

events.title: SF Mime Troupe: Wreckage at The Panhandle
events.description: Free outdoor performance of 'Wreckage: A Musical Tragicomedy' at the Panhandle (Lyon St between Oak & Fell). Live music warm-up at 1:30 PM.

events.title: Petty Theft: Tom Petty Tribute
events.description: San Francisco's premier Tom Petty tribute band delivers hits and deep cuts on the Won't Back Down Tour 2026. Doors at 8 PM.

events.title: SFMOMA: O. Smith Artivist — Opens
events.description: New exhibition 'O. Smith: Artivist' featuring works on paper by Orlando Smith chronicling the criminal legal system and abuses of power. On view through November 29, 2026.

events.title: Stern Grove: SF Symphony + Bela Fleck
events.description: The San Francisco Symphony teams up with banjo virtuoso Béla Fleck for an afternoon concert at Stern Grove. Free with lottery ticket. Doors noon, show 2 PM.

events.title: BATS Improv: Summer Cave Match (Student Show)
events.description: BATS 40th Summer Cave Match — competitive student improv showcase. Fort Mason Center, Building B.

events.title: Ferry Fest 2026
events.cost_notes: free with RSVP; 21+ wine tasting pass extra
events.description: Noise Pop and the SF Ferry Building present a free 6-hour outdoor music festival with Bay Area artists including French Cassettes, Combo Tezeta, Animal Prince, Grooblen, and more. Vintage vendors, art, and family activities. All ages.

events.title: The Shindig Show with Jimmy Shin
events.description: The Shindig Show with Jimmy Shin at Cobb's Comedy Club, 915 Columbus Ave.

events.title: White Denim
events.description: Austin psych-rock veterans White Denim perform with Arc Iris. Doors at 7 PM. All ages.

events.title: Corrosion of Conformity
events.cost_notes: $30 advance, $40 door; with Whores and Crobot
events.description: Legendary heavy rock band Corrosion of Conformity reunited with guitarist/vocalist Pepper Keenan — first North American tour since 2006. Support from Whores and Crobot. Doors at 6:30 PM.

events.title: Mohama Saz
events.description: Kurdish multi-instrumentalist Mohama Saz performs traditional and contemporary Kurdish music at The Chapel. Doors at 7 PM.

events.title: People in Plazas – Free Lunchtime Music
events.description: Free outdoor lunchtime concerts at plazas on and near Market Street. Runs Tuesdays and Thursdays through September 2026.

events.title: King Woman
events.description: Heavy, ethereal rock band King Woman at Great American Music Hall. Doors at 7 PM.

events.title: BATS Improv: Hitchcock Performance Lab (Student Show)
events.description: BATS student performance lab: genre-driven improvisation inspired by Hitchcock suspense tropes. Fort Mason Center, Building B.

events.title: People in Plazas – Free Lunchtime Music
events.description: Free outdoor lunchtime concerts at plazas on and near Market Street. Runs Tuesdays and Thursdays through September 2026.

events.title: Dead Souls: Joy Division Tribute
events.description: Dead Souls, San Francisco's acclaimed Joy Division and New Order tribute band. Doors at 8 PM.

events.title: Futurebirds
events.description: Athens, GA indie rock band Futurebirds at The Chapel. Doors at 8 PM.

events.title: LOVE SUPREME Summer Day Party
events.description: LOVE SUPREME Summer Day Party Series at The Chapel — afternoon dance party with DJ sets. All ages. 2 PM.

events.title: Memory Cradle
events.description: Ongoing through July 18 at Eleanor Harwood Gallery within Minnesota Street Project. Works exploring memory, material, and personal archive. Wed–Sat 11am–5pm.

events.title: ICA SF: Tara Donovan — Stratagems
events.description: Site-specific solo exhibition by Tara Donovan on view through July 31. Also on view: Jeffrey Gibson's 'This Burning World', Lily Kwong's 'Earthseed Dome' (through July 26), and Rupy C. Tut's 'The Picnic Potluck'.
